_about: Material tiebreaker for drawn games (MCTS value signal). A turn-cap draw scores cap*tanh((wv*dVillages + wg*dGold + wu*dUnitValue)/score_scale) from each side's perspective instead of a flat 0, so the value head gets gradient while the policy is too weak to win outright. See tools/draw_tiebreak.py for the full rationale.
_units: weight_unit_value 0.05 => 20 gold-worth of units == 1 village == 1 point (village = 2g/turn + upkeep support, ~10-turn horizon). weight_gold 0 since 2026-07-20: banked gold is worthless in MP Wesnoth (no carryover) and pricing it at par with units taught the policy to hoard (2.8x the SL prior, BACKLOG 2026-07-20) -- only UNITS score, so converting gold is strictly rewarded. score_scale 5.0 => a 5-point lead reaches 76% of cap. cap 0.3 keeps any draw far below a real win (+1).
